book_summary: Numbers has chapters 1-25 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The wilderness narrative now extends through Balak and Balaam, the speaking donkey, repeated transformation of purchased curse into blessing, Mah Tovu, the star-and-scepter and nations oracles, and the Peor/Phinehas crisis. Safeguards explicitly reject curse-for-hire spirituality, animal cruelty, antisemitism, ethnic supremacy, supersessionism, nationalist prophecy, zealot violence, misogyny, xenophobia, religious persecution, sexual shaming, collective punishment, and the use of plague as moral diagnosis.

chapters.notes: Balak's curse embassy, Balaam's restricted speech, Divine permission/anger tension, angel as adversary, speaking donkey, animal mistreatment, and first meeting with Balak.
First and second Balaam oracles, purchased curse transformed into blessing, distinct identity without supremacy, Exodus strength, and anti-divination claim.
Spirit upon Balaam, Mah Tovu, third blessing, star-and-scepter oracle, and violent nations sayings under anti-nationalist and anti-supersessionist safeguards.
Peor worship crisis, Zimri and Cozbi named, Phinehas' double killing, plague, covenant of peace, and anti-Midian command under maximum anti-violence safeguards.

translation_notes: Numbers 22's donkey sees danger before the seer and protests repeated violence; the animal's refusal preserves Balaam's life.
Balaam is a foreign diviner who nevertheless receives words and spirit from YHWH; Divine speech is not ethnically confined.
Royal honor, payment, repeated altars, and altered viewpoints cannot purchase a curse against the blessed people.
Numbers 23's 'dwelling apart' is poetic distinctness, not ethnic isolation or supremacy, and poetic innocence is not moral perfection.
Numbers 24:5, Mah Tovu, remains living Jewish liturgy and must not be superseded or appropriated against Jewish continuity.
The star-and-scepter oracle has layered Jewish, Christian, and political reception and is not collapsed into one later claimant.
Violent nations oracles are ancient royal-war rhetoric and cannot authorize modern ethnic, territorial, messianic, or nationalist violence.
Numbers 25 names both Zimri and Cozbi and must not make women the sole scapegoats for Israelite worship choices.
Phinehas' killing followed by a covenant of peace is morally dangerous reception material and may never authorize vigilantism, honor killing, persecution, or violence against couples.
Plague language must never be mapped onto sexually transmitted infection, epidemic, infertility, sexuality, or modern moral blame.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Numbers 22 Divine permission/anger sequence requires literary and source-history audit.
Numbers 22 angel-as-satan terminology requires ordinary-adversary versus later-figure distinction.
Numbers 22 speaking-donkey narrative requires animal-ethics and reception-history audit.
Numbers 23 'dwelling apart,' poetic innocence, wild-ox, and lion language require lexical and anti-supremacy audit.
Numbers 24 opened-eye wording, Agag reference, Mah Tovu reception, and star/scepter interpretation require textual and reception-history audit.
Numbers 24 final nations oracles require empire-history, poetic-source, and anti-nationalist safeguards.
Numbers 25 execution/exposure wording in verse 4 requires legal and lexical audit.
Numbers 25 Zimri-Cozbi chamber language requires sexual-interpretation uncertainty and anti-misogyny safeguards.
Numbers 25 Phinehas reception requires categorical anti-vigilantism, anti-honor-killing, and anti-religious-persecution metadata.
Numbers 25 Midian command must crosslink with Numbers 31 under explicit anti-genocide safeguards.
next_actions: Continue in a larger batch with Numbers 26-30: second census and inheritance framework, daughters of Zelophehad, Moses' succession request and Joshua commissioning, daily/Sabbath/monthly/festival offering calendar, and women's vow laws—with anti-militarization, gender-justice, anti-patriarchy, and anti-coercion safeguards.
